name: Commonwealth Co-Operative Bank
description_short: Commonwealth Cooperative Bank is Boston's oldest cooperative bank, established in 1886, offering personal and business banking with checking, savings, loans, and mortgages.
description_long:
Commonwealth Cooperative Bank has operated as a community-focused financial institution in the Boston area for over 135 years, since its founding in 1886. The bank positions itself as a traditional cooperative bank committed to serving its local community with personalized service and modern banking capabilities. Its longevity and cooperative structure reflect a commitment to member-customer relationships rather than external shareholders.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal banking products including savings accounts, checking accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, and safe deposit boxes. On the lending side, they provide consumer loans and mortgages. For business customers, Commonwealth offers business checking accounts, business money market accounts, sweep accounts, and commercial loans. Digital banking services include online banking, mobile banking, telephone banking, and debit card management through their Card Valet application, which allows customers to control debit card activity via smartphone.

Commonwealth distinguishes itself through its cooperative banking model, local roots in the Boston community (specifically Hyde Park and Jamaica Plain), and emphasis on personal service. The bank provides accessible customer support via phone (617-364-6000) and email (ccb@commonwealthcoop.com), maintains physical branch locations with extended hours, and offers robust online security resources covering topics like phishing, identity theft, ransomware, and telephone scams.

As a smaller regional cooperative bank, Commonwealth serves customers seeking community-oriented banking without the scale of national institutions. However, prospective customers should note the bank operates only two physical locations in the Boston area and may have more limited product sophistication or competitive rates compared to larger institutions. The website provides rates and fee information but does not display specific rate comparisons or promotional offerings.
services: Debit cards with Card Valet mobile app control
pros: Established cooperative bank with 135+ years of history and community presence in Boston
Two physical branch locations in Hyde Park and Jamaica Plain with extended hours including Saturday service
Free Card Valet mobile app for real-time debit card control and spending limits by category
Comprehensive personal and business banking services including mortgages, commercial loans, and money market accounts
24-hour ATM access at main office location (46 Fairmount Ave.)
Robust online security education covering phishing, identity theft, ransomware, and corporate account takeover
Multiple banking channels: online, mobile, telephone, and in-person
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only two branch locations in Boston area, restricting accessibility for non-local customers
Website does not display current interest rates or fee structures, requiring customers to contact bank directly for pricing information
No mention of FDIC insurance limits or account protection details on available pages
Limited information about loan approval processes, credit requirements, or product terms on public website
No evidence of online account opening functionality mentioned, potentially requiring in-person or phone-based application
best_for: Boston-area residents and businesses seeking relationship-based banking from a local cooperative institution
Customers prioritizing community bank values and cooperative ownership structure over national bank convenience
Small business owners needing commercial loans and business banking services in the Hyde Park or Jamaica Plain areas
diagnosis: Commonwealth Cooperative Bank is best for Boston-area residents and small businesses seeking personalized service from a community-oriented financial institution with deep local roots. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—with only two branch locations, the bank is not practical for customers outside the Boston metro area, and even local customers should verify that branch hours and service offerings meet their needs before opening an account.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
